# Design System Specifications

**Generated by**: design-nexus v3.0.0-enhanced  
**Source**: shadcn-ui Button component  
**Date**: 2024-04-24  
**Mode**: Soft Mode (single component validation)

---

## Directory Structure

This `/specs/` directory follows the **LLM-friendly three-tier architecture**:

```
/specs/
├── foundations/        # Tier 1: Design tokens
│   └── tokens.css      # CSS variables with 3-layer indirection
├── atoms/              # Tier 2a: Single-purpose components
│   └── Button.md       # Button component specification
├── molecules/          # Tier 2b: Composed components (none in this test)
├── organisms/          # Tier 2c: Complex components (none in this test)
└── patterns/           # Tier 3: Layout rules and composition guidance
    ├── layout-rules.md
    ├── responsive.md
    └── composition.md
```

---

## Component Categorization

Components are automatically categorized by complexity:

- **Atoms** (0-1 child types): Button, Input, Badge, etc.
- **Molecules** (2-3 child types): Card, Dropdown, Tooltip, etc.
- **Organisms** (4+ child types): DataTable, Modal, Form, etc.

**Button** is categorized as an **ATOM** (child types: TEXT, INSTANCE, VECTOR).

---

## How to Use These Specs

### For Designers
Read component specs to understand:
- When to use each component (and when NOT to use it)
- Component anatomy and constituent parts
- Design decisions and WHY context
- Token bindings and semantic naming

### For Developers
Implement from specs (not Figma screenshots):
- Props/API contracts define component behavior
- Token bindings map design → code
- Code examples provide implementation patterns
- Cross-references show composition relationships

### For AI Agents
Parse specs as structured context:
- Overview section prevents wrong component selection
- Anatomy section provides structural understanding
- Token bindings enable semantic token usage
- Code examples demonstrate correct implementation patterns

---

## Token Architecture (3-Layer Indirection)

This design system uses **3-layer CSS variable indirection**:

```css
/* Layer 1: Upstream tokens (--ds-*) */
--ds-slate-900: #0F172A;

/* Layer 2: Project aliases with fallbacks (--*) */
--color-primary: var(--ds-slate-900, #0F172A);

/* Layer 3: Component usage (references Layer 2 only) */
/* Documented in component specs, not in tokens.css */
```

**Why 3 layers?**
- Layer 1: Swappable upstream values (design system source)
- Layer 2: Project-specific names + fallbacks (prevents breakage)
- Layer 3: Component CSS only references Layer 2 (never Layer 1)

See `foundations/tokens.css` for full token definitions.

---

## Validation Results

**Component**: Button  
**Variants**: 19 total  
**shadcn/ui Compliance**: 0%  
**Violations**: 25 total

**Key Issues:**
- 0 Figma variables used (100% hardcoded colors)
- All variants have height=40px (need 44px minimum for accessibility)
- Non-standard variant names ("subtle" should be "secondary")
- Icon variants should use composition pattern instead of variant property

See `atoms/Button.md` for detailed audit results and recommendations.

---

## LLM-Friendly Alignment

This specification format is **75% aligned** with hvpandya.com LLM-friendly design system guidelines:

- ✅ Three-tier file hierarchy (foundations → atoms/molecules/organisms → patterns)
- ✅ 8-section component template (Overview, Anatomy, Props, States, Token Bindings, Code Examples, Cross-References)
- ✅ "When to use" guidance (prevents LLM fabrication)
- ✅ tokens.css with 3-layer indirection
- ✅ Pattern documentation (layout rules, responsive patterns, composition)

**Reference**: https://hvpandya.com/llm-design-systems
